title: Salaried Employee — Single Employer, No Other Income
description: The most common individual filing pattern in India. Taxpayer has salary income from one employer, possibly savings/FD interest, at most one self-occupied house property, and no other income. This pattern covers a large fraction of salaried employees in the formal sector.

prevalence.description: Covers the majority of salaried employees in organised sector employment.

typical_evidence.notes: Form 16 from single employer is the primary document. Part A (TRACES) verifies TDS. Part B provides salary breakdown and deductions.
26AS to verify TDS credit matches Form 16 Part A. Also verifies advance tax / SAT if any.
AIS to confirm no additional income exists (business receipts, capital gains, rent) beyond salary and interest.
Prefill JSON from IT Portal to auto-populate bank account, personal details, and AIS summary.

applicable_decisions.notes: With this pattern, D-001 will almost always resolve to ITR-1. Run anyway to confirm no disqualifiers.
Regime selection. For most taxpayers in this pattern, the New Regime is better unless deductions are substantial.
TDS credit. Cross-check Form 16 Part A against 26AS. This is the most likely source of error in this pattern.
Refund or tax due. For most in this pattern, refund arises from excess TDS by employer.

common_validations.notes: Employer TDS mismatch with 26AS is common. Employer may have made corrections mid-year that aren't reflected in Form 16.
FD interest in AIS is commonly missed by taxpayers who don't receive Form 16A from bank.
Always check bank pre-validation. Refunds are common in this pattern.

intake_questions.question: Did you receive salary from only one employer during FY 2024-25?
intake_questions.purpose: Confirm single employer. If multiple employers, upgrade to PAT-004.

intake_questions.question: Did you have any income other than salary and bank interest this year — for example, freelance work, stock market gains, rental income, or dividend income above ₹5,000?
intake_questions.purpose: Screen for other income heads that would change the pattern.

intake_questions.question: How many properties do you own or live in?
intake_questions.purpose: Confirm single house property. More than one may require ITR-2.

intake_questions.question: Is your total income from all sources likely to exceed ₹50 lakh this year?
Are you a director in any company (even a dormant or family company)?
intake_questions.purpose: Director flag disqualifies ITR-1 and ITR-4.

edge_cases.condition: Taxpayer changed jobs during the year — has salary from two employers
edge_cases.consequence: Two Form 16s. Combined salary must be declared. Employer 2 may not have known about Employer 1 income, causing under-TDS.

edge_cases.condition: Taxpayer has a let-out house property (not just SOP)
edge_cases.consequence: Rental income must be declared. ITR-1 supports only SOP or one let-out HP, but let-out requires additional schedule.
Dividend is taxable at slab rate from AY2021-22. Must be declared even if small. ITR-1 can accommodate this.

notes: This is the golden path pattern. If Jarviz works well for PAT-001, it covers approximately 45% of individual filers with high confidence and low complexity.
